meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no understanding of APIs (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no understanding of APIs. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: no understanding of APIs

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve API Gaps (Advanced Guide)
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing models to overcome API integration hurdles. A step-by-step advanced guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first app.
content.answer_block: To bridge your API knowledge gap, implement a tiered SaaS pricing model using Stripe's API. By architecting a usage-based billing system, you force yourself to learn API authentication, webhooks, and asynchronous event handling. This transforms theoretical pricing models into a functional, revenue-ready technical infrastructure for your portfolio.
content.intro:
The screen glows with a dozen dead-end tabs. You have the business logic, but the moment you try to connect your SaaS to a payment provider, the documentation feels like a cryptic map to a city that doesn't exist. You aren't just missing a feature; you are missing the fundamental language of the internet: the API.

Stop treating APIs like black boxes. By building a robust SaaS pricing engine, you stop being a student who 'makes things' and start becoming an engineer who 'ships products.' We are going to strip away the fear of endpoints and replace it with the ironclad confidence of a developer who knows exactly how data flows between their app and the global economy.

steps.title: Define the Tiered Pricing Schema
steps.description: Establish your pricing strategy (Freemium, Pro, Enterprise) within your database schema before touching any API code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a database schema in Prisma for a SaaS application with three tiers: Free, Pro, and Enterprise, including usage limits for API calls.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create schema.prisma with User, Subscription, and UsageLimit models'

steps.title: Initialize Stripe API Integration
steps.description: Configure your environment variables and Stripe SDK to handle secure server-side communications.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a boilerplate Stripe service module in TypeScript that initializes a customer and creates a subscription session.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'install stripe package and setup stripe.ts service file'

steps.title: Implement Webhook Listeners
steps.description: Learn to handle asynchronous events, the most critical skill for mastering API communication.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write an Express route to handle Stripe webhooks, specifically listening for 'checkout.session.completed' to update user subscription status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create webhook route to verify stripe signature and update database'

steps.title: Build Dynamic Usage Metering
steps.description: Connect your app logic to your pricing engine to track real-time usage consumption.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware function that checks if a user has exceeded their API call limit before allowing the request to proceed.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement usage-check middleware for API routes'

steps.title: Testing and Edge-Case Hardening
steps.description: Use simulation tools to ensure your pricing engine doesn't break during high-traffic or failed payment events.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test suite for the pricing engine covering scenarios like expired credit cards and subscription upgrades.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate jest tests for subscription service'

pro_tips: Always store currency in cents (integers) to avoid floating-point math errors in financial calculations.
Use idempotent keys for all payment API calls to prevent accidental double-billing during network retries.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your DB to prevent immediate service disruption on failed webhooks.
Never trust client-side pricing data; always re-verify the subscription tier on the server before granting access.
Use mock webhooks locally to test your integration logic without needing to trigger real Stripe events.
content.next_steps: Deploy your staging environment to Vercel and simulate a full user checkout flow from registration to subscription cancellation.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to build a scalable, monetized SaaS application. Don't let this knowledge sit idle in a folder—deploy it, test it, and scale it. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
